name: portkey
description: Read LLM gateway analytics from Portkey — cost, request, and token usage over time, per-user summaries, virtual keys, and log exports

envVars.name: PORTKEY_API_KEY
envVars.description: Portkey API key — analytics endpoints require an Admin API key (organization-wide) rather than a workspace key

tools.name: Get Cost Analytics
tools.description: Get time-series LLM spend data for a workspace over a date range, optionally filtered by model, status, or metadata

workspace_slug.description: Slug of the workspace to report on

time_of_generation_min.description: Start of the time range (ISO8601), e.g. 2026-06-01T00:00:00Z

time_of_generation_max.description: End of the time range (ISO8601), e.g. 2026-06-11T00:00:00Z

ai_org_model.description: Filter by provider__model combinations, comma-separated, e.g. openai__gpt-4o

status_code.description: Filter by response status codes, comma-separated

metadata.description: Stringified JSON object of metadata filters

tools.name: Get Request Analytics
tools.description: Get time-series request volume data for a workspace over a date range to spot traffic spikes and error trends

workspace_slug.description: Slug of the workspace to report on

time_of_generation_min.description: Start of the time range (ISO8601), e.g. 2026-06-01T00:00:00Z

time_of_generation_max.description: End of the time range (ISO8601), e.g. 2026-06-11T00:00:00Z

ai_org_model.description: Filter by provider__model combinations, comma-separated, e.g. openai__gpt-4o

status_code.description: Filter by response status codes, comma-separated

tools.name: Get Token Analytics
tools.description: Get time-series token consumption data for a workspace over a date range

workspace_slug.description: Slug of the workspace to report on

time_of_generation_min.description: Start of the time range (ISO8601), e.g. 2026-06-01T00:00:00Z

time_of_generation_max.description: End of the time range (ISO8601), e.g. 2026-06-11T00:00:00Z

ai_org_model.description: Filter by provider__model combinations, comma-separated, e.g. openai__gpt-4o

tools.name: Get User Analytics Summary
tools.description: Get per-user grouped analytics (request counts and cost) for a workspace over a date range

workspace_slug.description: Slug of the workspace to report on

time_of_generation_min.description: Start of the time range (ISO8601), e.g. 2026-06-01T00:00:00Z

time_of_generation_max.description: End of the time range (ISO8601), e.g. 2026-06-11T00:00:00Z

tools.name: Get Model Analytics Summary
tools.description: Get per-model grouped analytics (request counts) for a workspace over a date range — spend and traffic by model

workspace_slug.description: Slug of the workspace to report on

time_of_generation_min.description: Start of the time range (ISO8601), e.g. 2026-06-01T00:00:00Z

time_of_generation_max.description: End of the time range (ISO8601), e.g. 2026-06-11T00:00:00Z

tools.name: List Workspaces
tools.description: List the organization's workspaces with their IDs and slugs — feeds the workspace_slug every analytics tool needs

historicalSummary.omitted: defaults, usage limit, and rate limit policy details

tools.name: List Virtual Keys
tools.description: List virtual keys (managed provider credentials) with their status, usage limits, and expiry

historicalSummary.omitted: usage limit details and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: List Log Exports
tools.description: List log export tasks for a workspace (Portkey exposes gateway logs via exports rather than a direct list endpoint)

workspace_id.description: Optional ID of the workspace whose log exports to list; omit to list exports across the organization

prompts.title: LLM spend report
prompts.prompt: Pull my Portkey cost analytics for the last 30 days and summarize spend trends by model.

prompts.title: Gateway traffic overview
prompts.prompt: Show me Portkey request and token usage for the last week and flag any unusual spikes or error rates.

prompts.title: Virtual key audit
prompts.prompt: List my Portkey virtual keys and flag any that are exhausted or close to expiry.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Portkey setup

steps.title: Create a Portkey account
steps.description: Go to https://app.portkey.ai and sign up — the free developer plan is enough to test the gateway and analytics.

steps.title: Create an Admin API key
steps.description: In the Portkey dashboard, open API Keys (https://app.portkey.ai/api-keys) and create an Admin API key. Admin keys grant organization-wide access; workspace keys cannot call the analytics endpoints. You need the Organization Owner or Admin role.

steps.title: Store the key
steps.description: Add it to your .env file as PORTKEY_API_KEY=...

steps.title: Find your workspace slug
steps.description: The workspace slug is visible in the Portkey dashboard URL and workspace settings; analytics tools require it as the workspace_slug parameter.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run Get Cost Analytics with a recent date range. A 401 means the key is invalid; a 403 usually means you used a workspace key instead of an Admin key.

notes: Analytics only covers traffic routed through the Portkey gateway — direct provider calls won't appear
Time range parameters (time_of_generation_min/max) are required on all analytics endpoints and must be ISO8601 timestamps
The virtual keys API is deprecated in favor of the newer Providers API, but existing virtual keys still work and remain listable
